Description: Your line-height should be the base unit for ALL vertical spacing. If body text has line-height: 1.5 on 16px type (= 24px), spacing values should be multiples of 24px. This creates subconscious harmony—text and space share a mathematical foundation.
Do: Use line-height as base unit for spacing

Description: Use fewer sizes with more contrast. A 5-size system covers most needs. Popular ratios: 1.25 (major third), 1.333 (perfect fourth), 1.5 (perfect fifth).
Use ch units for character-based measure (max-width: 65ch). Line-height scales inversely with line length—narrow columns need tighter leading, wide columns need more.
Do: Use ch units for measure, adjust line-height by line length
Don't: Use fixed line-height regardless of measure

Description: Avoid the invisible defaults: Inter, Roboto, Open Sans, Lato, Montserrat. These are everywhere, making your design feel generic.
Do: Use distinctive fonts like Instrument Sans, Plus Jakarta Sans, Outfit, Onest, Figtree
Don't: Use overused fonts (Inter, Roboto, Arial, Open Sans)

Description: System fonts are underrated: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, system-ui looks native, loads instantly, and is highly readable.
Do: Consider system fonts for performance-critical apps

Description: You often don't need a second font. One well-chosen font family in multiple weights creates cleaner hierarchy than two competing typefaces.
Do: Use one font family in multiple weights, or pair with genuine contrast

Description: The layout shift problem: fonts load late, text reflows, and users see content jump.
Do: Use font-display: swap and match fallback metrics

Description: Use clamp(min, preferred, max) for fluid typography. The middle value (e.g., 5vw + 1rem) controls scaling rate.
Don't: Use fixed sizes or only vw units

Description: Most developers don't know these exist. Use them for polish: tabular numbers, proper fractions, small caps, kerning.
Never disable zoom: user-scalable=no breaks accessibility. If your layout breaks at 200% zoom, fix the layout.
Code Example Good: Allow zoom, fix layout for 200% zoom

Description: Use rem/em for font sizes: This respects user browser settings. Never px for body text.
Smaller than 16px strains eyes and fails WCAG on mobile.
